# Design System Doc Spec (DSDS)

> A machine-readable format for design system documentation. DSDS structures components, tokens, themes, foundations, patterns, and guides as a single source of truth for humans, parsers, and agents.

This site documents DSDS, a versioned JSON Schema. Every definition below has an HTML page (for people) and a `$defs` entry in the bundled schema below (for parsers/agents) — prefer the JSON when you just need field names, types, and requiredness.

## Machine-readable schema

- [Bundled schema, v0.15.2](https://designsystemdocspec.org/v0.15.2/dsds.bundled.schema.json): every definition in one JSON file
- [sitemap.xml](https://designsystemdocspec.org/sitemap.xml): every page on this site

## Guides

Each guide below also has a plain-markdown mirror at the same path with a `.md` extension (e.g. `/quickstart.md`) — the raw prose, no HTML/JS.

- [Design system doc spec 0.15.2](https://designsystemdocspec.org/): A machine-readable format for design system documentation. DSDS structures components, tokens, themes, foundations, patterns, and guides as a single source of truth for humans, parsers, and agents. ([markdown](https://designsystemdocspec.org/index.md))
- [DSDS quick start guide](https://designsystemdocspec.org/quickstart): Get started with DSDS — document structure, entity types, the document block system, and minimal examples you can copy. ([markdown](https://designsystemdocspec.org/quickstart.md))
- [DSDS for humans and agents](https://designsystemdocspec.org/humans-and-agents): A DSDS document serves two readers — people and AI agents. How documentBlocks and agentDocumentBlocks split the work between them. ([markdown](https://designsystemdocspec.org/humans-and-agents.md))
- [Schema architecture](https://designsystemdocspec.org/schema-architecture): The full DSDS schema reference — document structure, entity properties, document block types, and all shared models. ([markdown](https://designsystemdocspec.org/schema-architecture.md))
- [Conformance — DSDS 0.15.2](https://designsystemdocspec.org/conformance): Conformance classes, RFC 2119 requirement levels, enforcement tiers, and the generated index of every normative statement in the DSDS schemas. ([markdown](https://designsystemdocspec.org/conformance.md))
- [Interoperability — DSDS 0.15.2](https://designsystemdocspec.org/interoperability): How DSDS composes with the W3C Design Tokens format, Custom Elements Manifest, Storybook, and design tools — the documentation layer over existing sources of truth, not a replacement for them. ([markdown](https://designsystemdocspec.org/interoperability.md))
- [Stability and the road to 1.0 — DSDS 0.15.2](https://designsystemdocspec.org/stability): The DSDS stability contract: what pre-1.0 means, version semantics, the deprecation policy, the enumerated breaking-change backlog, and the criteria for declaring 1.0. ([markdown](https://designsystemdocspec.org/stability.md))
- [Migration — DSDS 0.15.2](https://designsystemdocspec.org/migration): How to move DSDS documents between spec versions: the 0.14.0 field-by-field mapping, the scripts that automate it, and the cases that need a human decision. ([markdown](https://designsystemdocspec.org/migration.md))

## Documentation

- [Design system doc spec (DSDS) v0.15.2](https://designsystemdocspec.org/root): DSDS is a machine-readable format for design system documentation. A DSDS document is either one entity (a component, token, pattern, and so on) or a group of entities. Each entity's `kind` says what it is: component, token, token-group, theme, foundation, pattern, guide, or chunk. Entity schemas live in entities/, document block schemas in document-blocks/, and shared schemas in common/.

## Common

- [Criterion definitions](https://designsystemdocspec.org/common-criterion): Definitions for testable success criteria, conformance, and external standard references. Criteria are the test-definition layer of DSDS documentation: each has a unique identifier with an objectively verifiable statement, an optional conformance `level`, a `verification` mode (automated, assisted, or manual), and an optional machine-dispatchable `check`. Test cases with declared outcomes make criteria self-verifying. Used by guideline entries and accessibility document blocks. Observed results belong in a guideline entry's `evidence`.
- [Dated note leaf definitions](https://designsystemdocspec.org/common-dated-note): Two shared building blocks for date-and-note metadata: `isoDate` (a date) and `plainNote` (a plain-text note). Reused by `lastUpdated`, `lastReviewed`, the status `note` (on the entity and per platform), and `docOrigin.note`.
- [Entity reference definitions](https://designsystemdocspec.org/common-entity-ref): References to other entities. `entityRef` is the standalone reference object; the `entityIdentifier` and `entityRole` defs are shared by `relationship`, use-case alternatives, and token overrides. Every identifier MUST match a documented entity. (Links, by contrast, address external resources by URL.)
- [Example definition](https://designsystemdocspec.org/common-example): The shared example schema used across DSDS. Each example shows a visual or interactive demo through a presentation and/or a literal value. Optional title and description add context. Presentations are typed objects defined in presentation.schema.json for images, videos, code snippets, and URLs. Other guideline items link to this as an `examples` array.
- [Extends definitions](https://designsystemdocspec.org/common-extends): Declares inheritance between DSDS documents and between entities, for a system inheriting from a parent system.
- [Extensions](https://designsystemdocspec.org/common-extensions): Vendor-specific extensions. An open object for tool metadata outside the core schema. Keys MUST use vendor namespaces.
- [Link definition](https://designsystemdocspec.org/common-link): A typed pointer to an external resource — source, design, docs, packages. Every link needs a `kind` and a `url`. Use the `relationships` array for references between entities instead of links.
- [Presentation definitions](https://designsystemdocspec.org/common-presentation): Typed presentation objects for visual and interactive demos: image, video, code snippet, or URL. Used by `example` and any schema that embeds a demo.
- [Relationship definitions](https://designsystemdocspec.org/common-relationship): How one entity relates to another (source → target). Relationships include: source depends on the target, souce is built from the target, source replaces the target, etc. Each relationship has a `relation` type and a target entity. You declare it on one entity, and tools work out the reverse direction. A relationship's target MUST be a documented entity. Use `link`, to point to external resources.
- [Rich text definition](https://designsystemdocspec.org/common-rich-text): A markdown text field for human-written documentation content, used across all DSDS schemas.
- [Status definition](https://designsystemdocspec.org/common-status): Shared status vocabulary. `statusValue` is the lifecycle string (draft, experimental, stable, deprecated, or custom). `platformStatus` captures one platform's readiness, and requires a `deprecationNotice` when that platform is deprecated.
- [System info definition](https://designsystemdocspec.org/common-system-info): The identity of the design system. Includes name, version, organization, URL, and license. Separate from the per-entity `metadata`.
- [Token overrides definition](https://designsystemdocspec.org/common-token-overrides): Shared token-override map used by anatomy parts, variant values, and states. Keys name the visual attribute; values reference the token used. Token references MUST name a documented token, never a raw value.
- [Use cases definition](https://designsystemdocspec.org/common-use-cases): When an artifact is the right choice and when another fits better. An optional `purpose` sums up what it is for; `items` holds scenarios marked 'recommended' or 'discouraged'.

## Entities

- [Chunk definitions](https://designsystemdocspec.org/entities-chunk): A chunk is a ready-to-use block of code — a copy-paste starting point built from the system's components, like a layout or a settings form. A chunk document holds its identity, the code itself, and general docs (guidelines, use-cases, accessibility, and so on). Chunks sit alongside components: a component documents one building block, a chunk documents a composition of them, captured as code.
- [Component definitions](https://designsystemdocspec.org/entities-component): A component document: the identity of a reusable UI element, plus its metadata (see metadata/metadata.schema.json) and its docs. `documentBlocks` accepts the component-specific kinds (imports, anatomy, api, variants, states, design-specifications) plus every general kind (guidelines, use-cases, accessibility, content, sections, checklist).
- [Foundation definitions](https://designsystemdocspec.org/entities-foundation): A foundation document covers a broad design domain a system is built on — color, typography, spacing, elevation, motion, shape, accessibility, content. It holds the domain's identity and metadata (see metadata/metadata.schema.json), plus its docs.
- [Guide definitions](https://designsystemdocspec.org/entities-guide): A guide is long-form guidance not tied to one component, token, foundation, or pattern — a getting-started walkthrough, contribution guide, tutorial, overview, or migration guide. It holds identity and metadata; the `category` field says what kind of guide it is. Its docs are sections, procedures (steps), guidelines, and use-cases.
- [Pattern definitions](https://designsystemdocspec.org/entities-pattern): A pattern is a repeated answer to a common UX problem — navigation, error messaging, form validation, empty states, loading, onboarding. A pattern document holds the identity of a multi-component solution, its metadata, and its docs.
- [Theme definitions](https://designsystemdocspec.org/entities-theme): A theme documents an alternative set of token values — a color mode, density setting, or brand variant. DSDS captures its purpose, docs, and relationships; the actual override values live in the DTCG file `source` points to. Put internal links in `links` metadata, not a block.
- [Token and token group definitions](https://designsystemdocspec.org/entities-token): Docs for individual tokens and token groups. Token values, aliases, and type live in the DTCG file — DSDS documents only the purpose, guidelines, and organization. A token group is a flexible unit: a full collection, a family (all color tokens), or a sub-family (one hue). Groups can hold tokens, nested groups, or both, forming a hierarchy. Both tokens and groups have an optional display `name` — when it's left out, the `identifier` serves as the label. Description and status are optional, to keep things terse at scale.

## Document blocks

- [Accessibility document block](https://designsystemdocspec.org/document-blocks-accessibility): Specialized, structured accessibility documentation: keyboard interactions, ARIA attributes, screen reader announcements, focus behaviors, color contrast pairs, and reduced-motion behaviors. Every entry is a verifiable statement of what the artifact does — a fact a test or review can confirm. For general documentation guidance — rules about what authors should do, with rationale and a conformance level — use a `guidelines` block (category 'accessibility').
- [Anatomy document block](https://designsystemdocspec.org/document-blocks-anatomy): Documents the visual structure of a component or pattern: its named sub-elements (parts), their design tokens, and optional annotated examples. Anatomy blocks break an artifact into the parts that designers and engineers need to understand, style, and test. For components, parts are rendered UI sub-elements (container, label, icon). For patterns, parts are sections of the pattern's visual layout (image, title, body, primary action).
- [API document block](https://designsystemdocspec.org/document-blocks-api): Documents the code-level interface of a component: configurable properties, emitted events, content slots, CSS custom properties, CSS shadow parts, data attributes, and public methods. When a component supports multiple platforms, each platform gets its own API block. The `platform` property says which implementation it describes.
- [Checklist document block](https://designsystemdocspec.org/document-blocks-checklist): A checklist of things to check off — built for agents, but useful for people too. Each item is a short, actionable line, with optional extra detail, a level that marks how strict it is, and an optional link to the criterion that proves it. Use `checklist` for a hands-on pass over something; use `guidelines` for the rules behind it.
- [Content document block](https://designsystemdocspec.org/document-blocks-content): Two kinds of content reference data: a dictionary of standard labels ('Add', 'Cancel', 'Delete') with definitions and usage notes, and localization notes (RTL, text expansion, pluralization, and other i18n concerns). At least one of `labels` or `localization` is required. For voice, tone, or capitalization rules, use a `guidelines` block (category 'content') instead.
- [Design specifications document block](https://designsystemdocspec.org/document-blocks-design-specifications): The visual specs of a component: property values, spacing, size limits, typography, and how it responds to breakpoints. Makes every visual decision explicit and measurable, so design and code stay in sync.
- [Document block — scoped unions](https://designsystemdocspec.org/document-blocks-document-blocks): Defines which document-block kinds each artifact type accepts. Every block has a `kind` tag; each artifact type accepts its own special kinds plus every general kind. We use a `kind` enum plus `if`/`then` branches instead of `oneOf`, so an invalid block gives one clear error for its `kind` instead of a pile of errors from every possible kind. To describe how entities relate to each other, use the `relationships` array on the entity, not a block kind; use `links` for external resources.
- [Guidelines document block](https://designsystemdocspec.org/document-blocks-guidelines): Usage rules for an artifact. Each item pairs a rule with why it exists. Guidelines say what to do (or not do), and why.
- [Imports document block](https://designsystemdocspec.org/document-blocks-imports): How to install and import a component in code, across platforms — also reused on guides for setup steps. One entry per platform (React, Vue, Web Components, etc.), each with the package name and the import code.
- [Interactions document block](https://designsystemdocspec.org/document-blocks-interactions): The step-by-step flow of a pattern: what triggers each step, what happens, and which components are involved. Read the items in order to follow the user's journey from start to finish.
- [Motion document block](https://designsystemdocspec.org/document-blocks-motion): The motion system for a foundation: named easing curves, how long to run them, and when to use each. Each item is one easing, with its curve, a recommended duration, and usage notes — enough for tools to generate CSS transitions or preview the curve.
- [Principles document block](https://designsystemdocspec.org/document-blocks-principles): The beliefs behind a foundation's decisions — each a short title plus what it means in practice. Principles answer 'what do we believe?' and sit above individual `guidelines`, shaping the whole approach to a domain (e.g., 'Functional first' for color, 'Use the scale' for spacing).
- [Scale document block](https://designsystemdocspec.org/document-blocks-scale): An ordered progression of values — a type scale, spacing scale, elevation scale, or similar. Each step links to a token, with an optional name, display value, and usage notes. A scale is the limited set designers and engineers choose from, instead of picking arbitrary values.
- [Sections document block](https://designsystemdocspec.org/document-blocks-sections): Free-form documentation content, organized into titled sections that can nest. Use it for anything that doesn't fit the structured blocks (api, anatomy, variants) — overviews, explanations, rationale. Each section has a heading, a body, examples, links, and optional sub-sections.
- [States document block](https://designsystemdocspec.org/document-blocks-states): Every interactive state a component can be in — hover, focus, disabled, loading, and so on. Each item covers what triggers it, how it looks and behaves, and which tokens change.
- [Steps document block](https://designsystemdocspec.org/document-blocks-steps): The steps a reader follows to complete a task, like getting started, a tutorial, or a migration. Each step has a short label, an instruction, optional examples, and an optional expected result so the reader knows it worked.
- [Variants document block](https://designsystemdocspec.org/document-blocks-variants): Every way a component can be configured — a toggle (flag variant, like 'disabled') or a set of options (enum variant, like size: sm | md | lg). Each can explain why it exists.

## Metadata

- [Aliases metadata field](https://designsystemdocspec.org/metadata-aliases): Alternative names for entities to support search, migration, and cross-referencing.
- [Category metadata field](https://designsystemdocspec.org/metadata-category): Where this entity fits in the design system's taxonomy.
- [Doc origin metadata field](https://designsystemdocspec.org/metadata-doc-origin): How this entity's documentation came to exist, tracked on two separate scales: `origin` (how the doc relates to the code — written first, generated from it, extracted from it, or reconstructed from memory) and `authorship` (who wrote the words — a person, an AI, a script, or some mix). Two documents can read identically but mean very different things: one extracted from shipped code by its maintainer, the other written from memory by an AI months later. Recording both lets tools and agents judge how much to trust the doc. A bare string sets the overall origin; the object form adds authorship, a note, and per-block overrides for documents with mixed origins.
- [Governance metadata field](https://designsystemdocspec.org/metadata-governance): Who's accountable for this entity's docs, and when someone last confirmed they're accurate. Answers 'who do I ask when the doc contradicts the code,' and lets tools flag docs nobody has checked in a while. Different from `lastUpdated`: that records when the doc changed; this records when someone confirmed it's still true. A doc untouched for a year but reviewed last month is healthy.
- [Last updated metadata field](https://designsystemdocspec.org/metadata-last-updated): When this entity's documentation last changed. A bare ISO date covers the common case; the object form adds a note describing what changed.
- [Links metadata field](https://designsystemdocspec.org/metadata-links): Links to external resources.
- [Entity metadata](https://designsystemdocspec.org/metadata-metadata): The metadata object every entity type accepts — a plain object keyed by field name. Each field has its own schema file in this directory (status, since, last-updated, category, tags, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, links, governance, doc-origin); `extends` points at the shared entityExtends definition. Every field is optional, and each can appear only once. Simple fields are just a value or array ('since': '1.0.0', 'tags': [...]); status, lastUpdated, and docOrigin take a short string for the common case or an object for more detail. An entity's `description` isn't metadata — it lives at the top level, next to `identifier` and `name`.
- [Preview metadata field](https://designsystemdocspec.org/metadata-preview): A visual or interactive preview.
- [Since metadata field](https://designsystemdocspec.org/metadata-since): The design system version in which this entity was introduced.
- [Status metadata field](https://designsystemdocspec.org/metadata-status): Lifecycle status of an entity. A bare string sets the overall status. The object form adds per-platform readiness, an optional explanatory note, and a deprecation notice.
- [Summary metadata field](https://designsystemdocspec.org/metadata-summary): A one-line plain-text summary for compact display contexts.
- [Tags metadata field](https://designsystemdocspec.org/metadata-tags): Freeform keywords for grouping, search, and cross-referencing.
- [Thumbnail metadata field](https://designsystemdocspec.org/metadata-thumbnail): A single image thumbnail for compact display, defined as a URL plus required alt text.
